principle: Keep thesis result simple. Trade result only updates when the user confirms a trade was taken.

rules.correct_thesis: Use only when the exact plan played out: reclaim/trigger, entry area, and TP.
rules.invalidated_thesis: Use when the planned trigger or entry was missed, never touched, or the setup failed before a valid entry.
rules.trade_taken: Only mark Yes when the user says they took the trade.
rules.trade_result: Only mark Win or Loss after the user confirms a trade was taken.

records.review_note: USUSDT analysis was inaccurate: price moved into the 4H supply zone, so the long/retest thesis should not receive correct-thesis credit.
HYPE did not come back to the mapped 65.85-66.15 entry zone or complete the 67.20 reclaim condition. Do not count the move as a correct thesis just because price was strong.
XRP moved after the valid entry trigger was missed. Do not count this as a correct thesis.

records.setup_type: Liquidity Sweep + Failed Reclaim / Supply Rejection
records.review_note: BREV short thesis worked through the planned entry/rejection idea and TP area. Trade result stays Not Taken until user confirms otherwise.
Reclaimed 0.0245 and moved to the TP1 area without ever retesting the entry zone. Missed entry, not a correct thesis.

records.setup_type: No Clean Intraday Setup / Deep Retest Watch
records.review_note: Unverified-catalyst pump retraced fully to the impulse origin. Long retest thesis wrong: sweep-fail signature at 0.0240 favored the short, and the zone had no base under it. No position, no loss.

milestones: TP Zone Validity Rule applied 07-15 - all three TPs recalculated to zone near-edges (0.6076 / 0.5934 / ~0.545), replacing the original 0.59/0.56/0.52
Corrected TP1 (0.6076) already tagged intraday during the 07-15 breakdown, before it was even relabeled as TP1
Closed manually 07-16 at 0.6051 - better than the corrected TP1 (0.6076), realized ~2.0R
records.review_note: User placed a live 10x isolated limit short at 0.6650 with SL 0.695 and TP 0.59, matching the analyzed zone and levels exactly - but the entry preceded confirmation, not the reverse. Reconciled via 15m candles and the Pine card's own ENTRY TAGGED timestamp (both agree, no timezone gap - user confirmed TradingView is also UTC+1): the 15:00 local candle (O 0.6506, H 0.6884, C 0.6747) tagged the entry zone while still climbing and closed near its high - no rejection yet. The actual rejection/confirmation candle was the next one, 15:15 local (O 0.6747, L 0.6521, C 0.6592). So the limit filled roughly 15-30 min before any confirmation existed. Entry timing was premature - that finding stands regardless of outcome. SEPARATE FINDING (07-15): the planned_tp above (0.59/0.56/0.52) was itself miscalibrated - it assumed price would clear a full demand zone (or two) with no evidence, a TP Zone Validity Rule violation caught and fixed in SETUP_LEDGER.json. Corrected targets became 0.6076/0.5934/~0.545 (each zone's near edge). RESOLUTION (07-16): user closed the position manually at 0.6051, entry 0.665, SL 0.695 never touched - realized R = (0.665-0.6051)/0.03 = ~1.997, essentially exactly 2R. 0.6051 is a better exit than the corrected TP1 (0.6076), so this clears TP1 on the corrected ladder even though it was a discretionary close rather than a limit fill. Graded WIN. Held ~3 days through two separate zone tests (a wick-only reclaim sweep above 0.68, and a sweep-and-reclaim of the TP1 zone itself) without the thesis ever structurally invalidating. Per the standing note above: this win does not retroactively excuse the premature entry or the three TP recalibrations - those remain logged as real process gaps (see SETUP_LEDGER.json journal, mistake tag PREMATURE_ENTRY_STILL_WON). Process and outcome are graded separately on purpose.

records.final_action: WAIT / SKIP (per the rules) - user took the trade anyway
records.outcome: Pending / Trade Open - taken below the R:R floor
milestones: Limit Filled on Retest 13:45 UTC+1 (0.0016469, resting order at 0.00165)
TP1 recalculated 07-15 to zone near-edge (0.0015244), dropping R:R from 2.4R to 1.26R - below the 2R Intraday floor
records.review_note: This is a genuinely different case from the JTO record above: entry TIMING and MECHANICS were correct here - the user waited for a real confirmed rejection candle (12:30 UTC+1, close 0.0016378 below the 0.00165 line, touched via a wick to 0.0016641 first), then let a resting limit at the zone edge (0.00165) get filled on the retest (13:45 UTC+1), rather than chasing market right after the reject candle. That is exactly the entry technique this workflow recommends. The problem is upstream of execution: TP1 was originally mis-mapped at 0.00141, skipping over a real, well-tested intervening demand zone (0.0014948-0.0015244, PUMPFUN's actual multi-day base) - a TP Zone Validity Rule violation caught only after the trade context came up in conversation. At the corrected, honest TP1 (0.0015244), this trade only pays 1.26R against the 2R Intraday floor - it should have been rated Skip/No Trade from the start (and was, once corrected), but the user had already entered before/independent of that correction landing. Do not credit this as a 'correct thesis' trade even if it closes in profit - the entry mechanics were sound but the underlying setup never should have been proposed as tradeable. When this closes, grade Win/Loss normally, but keep the R:R-floor-violation flagged separately from thesis-direction correctness - these are two different failure/success axes and conflating them would hide the real lesson (zone-mapping completeness, not entry discipline).
